name: Keep Alive (Response)
desc: Response to S00PacketKeepAlive. The client echoes back the server's keep-alive ID.

fields.name: key
fields.desc: The keep-alive ID received from the server

notes: If the client doesn't respond within ~30 seconds, the server will disconnect them.

implementation.overview: C00PacketKeepAlive is the client"s response to the server"s S00PacketKeepAlive. The server periodically sends a keepalive request with a random integer ID, and the client MUST echo that ID back within ~30 seconds or the server disconnects with "Timed out". This packet is one of the most commonly intercepted in 1.8.9 clients because it directly controls how the anti-cheat measures connection latency and packet timing. Intercepting or delaying it is the foundation of PingSpoof, many Disabler modes, and Timer/LagSwitch modules.

modules.name: PingSpoof
modules.purpose: Fakes higher ping by delaying the keepalive response. Useful for looking like you have lag (to explain away impossible angles/fast reactions) or for anti-cheat bypass.
modules.how_it_works: When S00PacketKeepAlive arrives (incoming), the module cancels the normal handler and saves the keepalive ID. Then instead of immediately sending C00 back, it waits a configurable delay (e.g. 500ms - 3000ms). After the timer fires, it sends C00PacketKeepAlive with the stored ID back. Some clients modify the ID by adding a random offset to make it look "natural".
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleKeepAlive() — the method that receives S00 and sends back C00. Override via EventReceivePacket before this method runs.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Vanilla timeout is ~30 seconds. Most anti-cheats (Watchdog, AAC, NCP) check that the lag is consistent — if you delay exactly 500ms every time, they flag it. Smart PingSpoof modules randomize the delay by ±15%. Some anti-cheats also check that the keepalive ID matches what they sent; random modifications to the ID can trigger flags.

modules.name: Disabler — Watchdog (Hypixel) mode
modules.purpose: Hypixel"s Watchdog anti-cheat uses keepalive timing + transaction (C0F) ordering to detect cheat modules. By queueing C00+C0F together and releasing them after a precise delay, the module "disables" Watchdog"s ability to correlate movement vs server position.
modules.how_it_works: The disabler intercepts BOTH C00PacketKeepAlive and C0FPacketConfirmTransaction. For the first 350ms of a movement burst, all C00 and C0F packets are cancelled and saved to a queue. After 350ms, ALL queued packets are flushed at once. This creates a burst of "valid" transactions that saturate Watchdog"s buffer, causing it to stop checking movement legitimacy.
modules.vanilla_hook: The keepalive ID flows through: NetworkManager.sendPacket() → EventSendPacket → Netty pipeline. Cancelling in EventSendPacket is the cleanest approach.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Watchdog: this technique (queue + burst) works because Watchdog uses a sliding window of ~500ms. By withholding all data for 350ms and then sending it all at once, the server-side check sees a "snapshot" of valid transactions. AAC: queues keepalive and transaction together, drops every Nth one.

modules.name: Disabler — Faithful / Erisium / Mineplex mode
modules.purpose: Cancels C00 entirely or replaces it with a corruted key to confuse specific anti-cheats.
modules.how_it_works: Faithful: cancel all C00 outgoing packets. The server never receives a response, but other tricks (like movement desync) keep the connection alive. Erisium: cancel and re-send with a random key. Mineplex: modify the key value by subtracting a random amount each time, making the keepalive look like it"s drifting.
modules.vanilla_hook: Same pattern — EventSendPacket. The key field on C00PacketKeepAlive can be modified via reflection or direct field access in MCP (the field is literally named "key" in 1.8.9).
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Faithful: cancel all keepalives and rely on movement syncing. Works on servers that only check keepalive for timeout (not anticheat). Mineplex: shifting the key exploits a bug in Mineplex"s anticheat where it accepts a range of values around the expected key. Modern anti-cheats expect an exact match.

modules.name: DialUpPing / LagSwitch
modules.purpose: Causes deliberate lag by dropping keepalive packets. Makes the player appear "laggy" to avoid suspicion or triggers a temporary server freeze.
modules.how_it_works: Every Nth keepalive packet is dropped entirely. The server eventually sees a timeout disconnect, but if the module starts sending again before the 30-second timeout, the connection survives. The player appears to have high packet loss.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Easy to detect server-side by checking packet loss ratio. Most ghost clients don"t use this; it"s a free/leisure client feature.

implementation.general_hooks:
There are THREE primary ways to intercept this packet in 1.8.9 clients:
1. EventSendPacket/EventReceivePacket — Most common. The client has a mixin or edited NetworkManager that fires events before/after every packet. You check instanceof C00PacketKeepAlive or S00PacketKeepAlive.
2. NetHandlerPlayClient.handleKeepAlive() — Override the specific handler method. Only works for incoming S00, but gives you direct access.
3. NetworkManager.sendPacket() — Modify the sendPacket method to filter or queue C00 packets globally. This is how Blink and similar modules often work at the lowest level.
implementation.client_variations:
Astro 2.2.1: Uses EventReceivePacket, modifies the keepalive ID by adding mc.getDebugFPS()/2 as offset.
Sigma 3.9: Full queue-based PingSpoof with configurable waitSeconds. Also has Disabler mode that completely cancels C00.
Tenacity 5.1: Integrates C00 handling into the Watchdog disabler — queues C00 alongside C0F, flushes after 350ms.
Lycoris 2.0: Sends a completely spoofed C00 with Integer.MIN_VALUE key for Faithful bypass.
November 0.2: Uses a timestamp-indexed queue (TreeMap with System.currentTimeMillis() + delay as key).
Memeware 7.3: Direct field mutation on the packet object (packetKeepAlive.key -= random bits).
Raze v1.5b: Drops C00 entirely as part of a hypixel fast disabler (allows only every Nth C03).
Jigsaw 0.24: Every 150th keepalive is dropped to simulate dial-up ping.
